title: Git Mental Model, Setup, Help, and Repository Safety · Independent Repository Trial
summary: Adopt an unfamiliar repository without overwriting identity, hooks, ignored files, or team policy. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain Git history as content-addressed snapshots connected by parent links and named references.
Distinguish working tree, Git directory, common directory, bare repository, linked worktree, and nested repository boundaries.
Inspect system, global, local, worktree, command, and environment configuration with origin and scope evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

lab case — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 8.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Help Version Safety: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Repository /home/learner/lab uses branch main, local author scope, and Git 7.x. Identify working tree, Git directory, config origins, installed help, and recovery reference before adopting it. steps.why: Retrieving Help Version Safety through the concrete a repository orientation and configuration evidence notebook case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use installed help, version evidence, dry runs, status, and backups before following repository-changing instructions.
